You can execute bash commands and edit files to implement the necessary changes.

## Recommended Workflow

This workflows should be done step-by-step so that you can iterate on your changes and any possible problems.

1. Analyze the codebase by finding and reading relevant files
2. Edit the source code to resolve the issue
3. Verify your fix works by running the `submit-patch` command
4. Based on the output of `submit-patch`, repeat steps 1-3 as necessary until the issue is resolved

## Important Rules

1. Every response must contain exactly one action
2. The action must be enclosed in triple backticks
3. Directory or environment variable changes are not persistent. Every action is executed in a new subshell.
   However, you can prefix any action with `MY_ENV_VAR=MY_VALUE cd /path/to/working/dir && ...` or write/load environment variables from files
4. Solving this issue does not require deleting any files or accessing the internet, so you are not permitted to use tools such as: {{forbidden_tools}}.

<system_information>
{{system}} {{release}} {{version}} {{machine}}
</system_information>

## Formatting your response

Here is an example of a correct response:

<example_response>
THOUGHT: I need to understand the structure of the repository first. Let me check what files are in the current directory to get a better understanding of the codebase.

```bash
ls -la
```
</example_response>

## Useful command examples

### Create a new file:

```bash
cat <<'EOF' > newfile.py
import numpy as np
hello = "world"
print(hello)
EOF
```

### Edit files with sed:

{%- if system == "Darwin" -%}
<important>
You are on MacOS. For all the below examples, you need to use `sed -i ''` instead of `sed -i`.
</important>
{%- endif -%}

```bash
# Replace all occurrences
sed -i 's/old_string/new_string/g' filename.py

# Replace only first occurrence
sed -i 's/old_string/new_string/' filename.py

# Replace first occurrence on line 1
sed -i '1s/old_string/new_string/' filename.py

# Replace all occurrences in lines 1-10
sed -i '1,10s/old_string/new_string/g' filename.py
```

### View file content:

```bash
# View specific lines with numbers
nl -ba filename.py | sed -n '10,20p'
```

### Submit your patch:

```bash
# This command has no arguments
submit-patch
```

### Any other permitted command you want to run:

```bash
anything
```

</warning><output_head>
   420	
   421	  return nullptr;
   422	}
   423	
   424	namespace {
   425	
   426	/// Recursive helper to read bits out of global. C is the constant being copied
   427	/// out of. ByteOffset is an offset into C. CurPtr is the pointer to copy
   428	/// results into and BytesLeft is the number of bytes left in
   429	/// the CurPtr buffer. DL is the DataLayout.
   430	bool ReadDataFromGlobal(Constant *C, uint64_t ByteOffset, unsigned char *CurPtr,
   431	                        unsigned BytesLeft, const DataLayout &DL) {
   432	  assert(ByteOffset <= DL.getTypeAllocSize(C->getType()) &&
   433	         "Out of range access");
   434	
   435	  // If this element is zero or undefined, we can just return since *CurPtr is
   436	  // zero initialized.
   437	  if (isa<ConstantAggregateZero>(C) || isa<UndefValue>(C))
   438	    return true;
   439	
   440	  if (auto *CI = dyn_cast<ConstantInt>(C)) {
   441	    if ((CI->getBitWidth() & 7) != 0)
   442	      return false;
   443	    const APInt &Val = CI->getValue();
   444	    unsigned IntBytes = unsigned(CI->getBitWidth()/8);
   445	
   446	    for (unsigned i = 0; i != BytesLeft && ByteOffset != IntBytes; ++i) {
   447	      unsigned n = ByteOffset;
   448	      if (!DL.isLittleEndian())
   449	        n = IntBytes - n - 1;
   450	      CurPtr[i] = Val.extractBits(8, n * 8).getZExtValue();
   451	      ++ByteOffset;
   452	    }
   453	    return true;
   454	  }
   455	
   456	  if (auto *CFP = dyn_cast<ConstantFP>(C)) {
   457	    if (CFP->getType()->isDoubleTy()) {
   458	      C = FoldBitCast(C, Type::getInt64Ty(C->getContext()), DL);
   459	      return ReadDataFromGlobal(C, ByteOffset, CurPtr, BytesLeft, DL);
   460	    }
   461	    if (CFP->getType()->isFloatTy()){
   462	      C = FoldBitCast(C, Type::getInt32Ty(C->getContext()), DL);
   463	      return ReadDataFromGlobal(C, ByteOffset, CurPtr, BytesLeft, DL);
   464	    }
   465	    if (CFP->getType()->isHalfTy()){
   466	      C = FoldBitCast(C, Type::getInt16Ty(C->getContext()), DL);
   467	      return ReadDataFromGlobal(C, ByteOffset, CurPtr, BytesLeft, DL);
   468	    }
   469	    return false;
   470	  }
   471	
   472	  if (auto *CS = dyn_cast<ConstantStruct>(C)) {
   473	    const StructLayout *SL = DL.getStructLayout(CS->getType());
   474	    unsigned Index = SL->getElementContainingOffset(ByteOffset);
   475	    uint64_t CurEltOffset = SL->getElementOffset(Index);
   476	    ByteOffset -= CurEltOffset;
   477	
   478	    while (true) {
   479	      // If the element access is to the element itself and not to tail padding,
   480	      // read the bytes from the element.
   481	      uint64_t EltSize = DL.getTypeAllocSize(CS->getOperand(Index)->getType());
   482	
   483	      if (ByteOffset < EltSize &&
   484	          !ReadDataFromGlobal(CS->getOperand(Index), ByteOffset, CurPtr,
   485	                              BytesLeft, DL))
   486	        return false;
   487	
   488	      ++Index;
   489	
   490	      // Check to see if we read from the last struct element, if so we're done.
   491	      if (Index == CS->getType()->getNumElements())
   492	        return true;
   493	
   494	      // If we read all of the bytes we needed from this element we're done.
   495	      uint64_t NextEltOffset = SL->getElementOffset(Index);
   496

   709	Constant *llvm::ConstantFoldLoadFromConst(Constant *C, Type *Ty,
   710	                                          const APInt &Offset,
   711	                                          const DataLayout &DL) {
   712	  if (Constant *AtOffset = getConstantAtOffset(C, Offset, DL))
   713	    if (Constant *Result = ConstantFoldLoadThroughBitcast(AtOffset, Ty, DL))
   714	      return Result;
   715	
   716	  // Explicitly check for out-of-bounds access, so we return poison even if the
   717	  // constant is a uniform value.
   718	  TypeSize Size = DL.getTypeAllocSize(C->getType());
   719	  if (!Size.isScalable() && Offset.sge(Size.getFixedValue()))
   720	    return PoisonValue::get(Ty);
   721	
   722	  // Try an offset-independent fold of a uniform value.
   723	  if (Constant *Result = ConstantFoldLoadFromUniformValue(C, Ty, DL))
   724	    return Result;
   725	
   726	  // Try hard to fold loads from bitcasted strange and non-type-safe things.
   727	  if (Offset.getSignificantBits() <= 64)
   728	    if (Constant *Result =
   729	            FoldReinterpretLoadFromConst(C, Ty, Offset.getSExtValue(), DL))
   730	      return Result;
   731	
   732	  return nullptr;
   733	}
   734	
   735	Constant *llvm::ConstantFoldLoadFromConst(Constant *C, Type *Ty,
   736	                                          const DataLayout &DL) {
   737	  return ConstantFoldLoadFromConst(C, Ty, APInt(64, 0), DL);
   738	}
   739	
   740	Constant *llvm::ConstantFoldLoadFromConstPtr(Constant *C, Type *Ty,
   741	                                             APInt Offset,
   742	                                             const DataLayout &DL) {
   743	  // We can only fold loads from constant globals with a definitive initializer.
   744	  // Check this upfront, to skip expensive offset calculations.
   745	  auto *GV = dyn_cast<GlobalVariable>(getUnderlyingObject(C));
   746	  if (!GV || !GV->isConstant() || !GV->hasDefinitiveInitializer())
   747	    return nullptr;
   748	
   749	  C = cast<Constant>(C->stripAndAccumulateConstantOffsets(
   750	          DL, Offset, /* AllowNonInbounds */ true));
   751	
   752	  if (C == GV)
   753	    if (Constant *Result = ConstantFoldLoadFromConst(GV->getInitializer(), Ty,
   754	                                                     Offset, DL))
   755	      return Result;
   756	
   757	  // If this load comes from anywhere in a uniform constant global, the value
   758	  // is always the same, regardless of the loaded offset.
   759	  return ConstantFoldLoadFromUniformValue(GV->getInitializer(), Ty, DL);
   760	}
   761

   765	  return ConstantFoldLoadFromConstPtr(C, Ty, std::move(Offset), DL);

   770	  if (isa<PoisonValue>(C))

   783	  return nullptr;

Here are some thoughts about why you want to perform the action.
The crash occurs when ReadDataFromGlobal processes a ConstantInt and ByteOffset exceeds the integer size in bytes. The loop condition allows ByteOffset > IntBytes, leading to APInt::extractBits with an out-of-range bit position. We will add an early return if ByteOffset >= IntBytes and tighten the loop condition to ByteOffset < IntBytes to prevent illegal extraction. Then we submit the patch.

```bash
perl -0777 -i -pe 's/(unsigned IntBytes = unsigned\(CI->getBitWidth\/8\);\s*\n\s*)(\n?)/$1    if (ByteOffset >= IntBytes) {\n      return true;\n    }\n/s; s/for \(unsigned i = 0; i != BytesLeft && ByteOffset != IntBytes; \+\+i\)/for (unsigned i = 0; i != BytesLeft && ByteOffset < IntBytes; ++i)/' llvm/lib/Analysis/ConstantFolding.cpp && submit-patch
```

The crash occurs when ReadDataFromGlobal processes a ConstantInt and ByteOffset exceeds the integer size in bytes. We will add an early return if ByteOffset >= IntBytes and tighten the loop condition to ByteOffset < IntBytes to prevent illegal extraction. Then we submit the patch.

